entries.answer: Goal: finish with the LOWEST score. Each card is worth its rank in points, so trim high cards and call Dutch when you think you are ahead. Discarding ALL your cards wins instantly.
Setup: each player gets 4 cards face down. At the very start you may secretly peek at 2 of your own cards. Memorise the low ones!
Initial peek: click 2 of YOUR cards to look at them secretly. They flip back down right away, so remember which ones are low value.
On your turn: draw a card from the deck first. Then either DISCARD it, or SWAP it with one of your face-down cards. Then end your turn.
Swap puts the drawn card into your hand and sends your old face-down card to the discard. Discard throws the drawn card straight away. Swap in low cards, discard high ones.

patterns: how much is a card worth
entries.answer: Card points equal the rank: Ace = 1 up to King = 13. The King of Diamonds is special and counts as 0. Lowest total wins.
The King of Diamonds counts as 0 points, the best card to keep. It only helps if it is in your hand at the end.
Queen: when you discard a Queen you may peek at ANY one face-down card on the table, yours or an opponent's. Use it to scout low cards.
Jack: when you discard a Jack you may blindly swap any TWO cards on the table. Nobody sees what moved, so trade your worst card for someone else's.
Jump-In: if you have a card of the SAME rank as the top discard, you can play it at any time, even out of turn. Match = your hand shrinks. Wrong card = penalty card plus you drink a beer.
Call Dutch when you think you have the lowest score. Everyone gets one last turn, then you CONFIRM to end the game or FORFEIT (and you cannot call Dutch again).
Beers are your lives, you start with 3. You drink one for a failed Jump-In or for instantly discarding a drawn card without using it. Drink all 3 and you are eliminated.
When you drink your 3rd beer you pass out and are eliminated for the rest of the game. Avoid reckless Jump-Ins and pointless instant discards.
You earn money by discarding cards, based on their value. Aces pay a lot; the King of Spades, Hearts and Clubs pay nothing. Spend money at the chicken to buy abilities.
A chicken hovers over the table. Click it (its legs) on your turn to spend money and get a random Ability Card egg. Abilities are kept face down until you use them.
Abilities (bought from the chicken): Bottoms Up, Refuel, Trim Off, Boulder, Uno Reverse, Skip, Perfect Match, Inflation, Half Off, Jumpscare, Shuffle, Polarity Shift. Ask about any one for details.
Bottoms Up: force a chosen player to drink a beer, pushing them one step closer to elimination.
Refuel: gain an extra beer (up to a maximum of 3). Great for staying alive longer.
Trim Off: remove your highest-value card from your hand for an instant score drop. Very strong.
Boulder: give a chosen player the highest card currently left in the deck. Their problem now.
Uno Reverse: reverse the turn order. Handy to make a dangerous opponent wait longer.
Skip: block a chosen player's next turn entirely. They sit it out.
Perfect Match: resets hands. You receive Ace, 2, 3, 4 (very low) while others get random cards. Everyone keeps their money and abilities.
Inflation: double a chosen player's card values for scoring only. The original rank still counts for Jump-Ins.
Half Off: halve a chosen player's card values for scoring only. Use it on yourself for an instant advantage.
Jumpscare: you receive a card, but a chosen player gets jumpscared and one of their cards flips, revealing what they were hiding.
Shuffle: randomly shuffle all of a chosen player's cards, ruining their carefully memorised hand.
Polarity Shift: invert the win condition so the HIGHEST score wins. A total game-changer if you are losing.
If you get rid of ALL your cards (mostly through Jump-Ins and swaps), you win the game immediately.
Use the EMOTE button (or T) at the bottom-right to open the emote wheel, then click one or press 1-4 to send a reaction to the table.
Default keys: R draw, X discard the drawn card, A/E select a card, Q confirm, Enter end turn, Space Jump-In, D call Dutch, C confirm Dutch, F forfeit, T emotes. You can rebind them in Settings.
